author: Sanjay Nikam
title: AirPrint Issue After Recent Update
content:
I have been using HP Laser MFP 138fnw and HP Smart Tank 675 printers for the last 2 to 3 years. Earlier, AirPrint from my iPhone 14 Pro Max was working perfectly with both printers.

However, after the recent update, I am facing a serious AirPrint issue. When I give a print command, the print does not come out. This problem does not appear to be related to the iPhone. It seems to be connected with the HP printer, Wi-Fi network connection, AirPrint connection, or the latest update.

I have already checked all the required settings, including Wi-Fi network, printer connection, HP Smart App, AirPrint support, printer online status, router, and iPhone settings. Both the iPhone and printers are connected to the same Wi-Fi network. These same settings were working properly earlier.

Therefore, HP should urgently look into this AirPrint issue caused after the recent update and provide a proper firmware/software update or technical solution. Regular HP customers are facing inconvenience because of this issue.

Overall Experience:
The printer quality is good, but after the recent update, the AirPrint problem is causing difficulty in daily use. I expect HP to resolve this issue as soon as possible.

author: Insta-ink sucks
title: Overcharging
content:
The printer works great. Really enjoy the app. My complaint is that I should not have to pay HP for every page I print. That’s ridiculous, and it gets expensive. It’s just another way for HP (and other like-companies) to suck the common person dry while raking in the billions. It was never like this growing up. I bought the printer, paper, and ink, and printed my hearts desire until the paper or ink ran out. 

And I realize that’s still an option, but the ink cartridges you sell in the store are expensive and only last about 50 pages and they’re done! You basically give me no choice but to sign up for your insta-ink BS because in the long run it actually ends up being a little cheaper than buying the cartridges, which isn’t saying much. 

All I want HP to do is sell me a printer and ink and then I’ll go my merry way. I don’t want to be hooked on your insta-ink dependency chain.

author: SpartacusMills
title: App nonsense, can’t connect, ink usage astronomical
content: Spent most of the day with a 6100e Envy. Downloaded an app in the process. Despite multiple attempts to connect to HP I am met with an error screen. “Unable to register the printer to your account”. On it goes. Regardless, I have got the thing to print (but haven’t managed to connect to the much touted instant ink. The printer itself  does the job well. But then I looked at the confounded app (which appears to be a data harvester). After printing 48 pages in black & white my black cartridge has about a quarter of ink remaining. Have printed off 12 pages of colour & the colour ink is down by a quarter. No wonder they demand your details for this instant ink racket. Will be returning the printer. Which is a shame, as it appears solid & does the job nicely. But I won’t be buying HP products.

author: Keither44
title: New 2026 HP Envy printer vs old 2018? Envy HP printer
content:
I’ve delayed getting a new printer for the last couple of years because the old one worked so well. But since the old one is no longer supported by HP, although it still worked fine, I bought this new one.

What a dramatic improvement in technology!

What I expected to be a complicated new printer setup, whatever complications are objectively inherent in it, all I had to do was push one button at a time and response to a single instruction at a time.

After around 15 of these pushes, the printer set up is completely resolved, and has just enabled me to seamlessly print from even my iPhone for the first time!

The voice recognition system has so far been exactly accurate! What a wonderful addition to the experience!

As a partially literate, 81-year-old printer enthusiast, I’m astonished at this “new“ innovation.

My longtime, unfailing faith in HP has been confirmed yet again.

Thanks to all the engineers, technicians, marketers, and other members of your corporation’s staff for bringing me this culminating pleasure to smooth out my last years of life.

What would we do without each other – the product producers and their grateful consumers?

Thanks to all of you for such a wonderful product!

author: Aarqe
title: I’d rate it lower if I could
content:
I have never hated a printer as much as I hate the HP DeskJet 2900. This app is a waste of time as it also could not wrangle the useless device. 

This printer managed to waste over an hour of my life the night before an important job interview. What should have been a simple task turned into an endless cycle of connection issues, error messages, setup problems, and frustration. Every time I thought I had fixed one problem, another appeared.

I’ve owned plenty of printers over the years and understand that technology isn’t always perfect, but this thing tested my patience in ways I didn’t think were possible. By the end of it, I was genuinely angry at a printer—which is not something I ever thought I’d say.

The print quality is irrelevant when actually getting the printer to do its job feels like a full-time occupation. A printer should make life easier, not turn a five-minute task into a stress-inducing nightmare.

I would not recommend the HP DeskJet 2900 to anyone. If your goal is to print documents quickly and without drama, look elsewhere. This is, without question, one of the most frustrating products I have ever purchased.

author: pattongl
title: HP is Always There for Me
content:
As the use of AI moves into more and more aspects of our daily lives, it is becoming more and more difficult to reach a live person when calling a large corporation for customer service for just about any product one can imagine. While I am not totally naïve when it comes to the use of my phone and computer, I do experience frequent problems with which I need assistance.  Often it’s very, very difficult to reach a live person. 
This has not been my experience with HP. For over 20 years I have used  HP home printers due to the fact that I can always reach a live person with a minimum of time and difficulty who can then solve my problem.  In addition, the HP products themselves are very dependable as my problems virtually always originate from my low level of technical skills. 

My HP home printers have invariably functioned for many years with almost no problems integral to the printers themselves, and I have no reservations whatsoever with recommending HP products to my friends and relatives. The HP printers are economical, generally trouble free for many years, and customer service with a live person who is friendly, courteous and highly knowledgeable is easy to reach 24/7.
